text: For ‘by the seventh day’ the Septuagint Greek has ‘on the sixth day’.
There is, here, a play upon words in the Hebrew; Adam, man, was made out of the ground, adamah. In the Latin text, the word Adam is translated ‘the man’ up to verse 18, and ‘Adam’ thenceforward.
The Hebrew text probably means that God planted a garden out in the East, in, Eden.
The Latin here translates the word Eden, as in verse 8 above.
It is not certain whether these words are represented as having been spoken by Adam, or whether they are a commentary by the author. Cf. Mt. 19.5.
